p.em: Engineers' Reference for epoll(7) and kqueue(2)

ul.li: LT: Can use blocking or non-blocking FDs. Simple programming model.
ET: MUST read/write until EAGAIN or EWOULDBLOCK.

div.p: The kernel notifies the process as long as the condition is true (e.g., data is present in the RX buffer).
The kernel notifies the process only when the state changes (e.g., from Empty to Not-Empty).

g.text: Buffer: [XXX ] (80 bytes remains) | Result: epoll_wait returns ✅ IMMEDIATELY
Condition still met. epoll_wait will keep firing until 0 bytes.
Buffer: [XXX ] (80 bytes remains) | Result: epoll_wait blocks ⏸️

text.tspan: Process must loop read() until EAGAIN
or it will never be notified again for these 80B!

g.text: Buffer: [XXXXXX] (180 bytes) | Result: epoll_wait returns ✅ (New Edge)
